mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and corporate operating leverage, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and SPX 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.0% signals an accommodative monetary stance that stimulates risk appetite and compresses equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Mean SPX 60d return following months with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) across a 10-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in payroll employment and decline in unemployment rate produce a labor market heat interaction that amplifies wage income growth and consumption forecasts beyond additive effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In a 3-year rolling regression predicting 20d SPX returns, the PAYEMS×UNRATE interaction term carries a non-significant coefficient (two-sided p > 0.05) or the estimated sign is negative.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, declining housing starts reflect deteriorating forward demand and tightening credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In periods classified as late-cycle expansions, a 3-month decline in HOUST is followed by positive 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI acceleration above 0.4% signals persistent inflation that erodes real earnings expectations and forces tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in greater than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL 1-month change exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail sales directly reflects consumer spending strength, the largest component of GDP, and positively predicts near-term S&P 500 returns through revenue surprises.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 1-month change and 5d SPX returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over two months signals recessionary labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over two months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding retail sales growth signals inventory accumulation and margin pressure that depresses earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO growth and RSAFS growth does not significantly predict 60d SPX returns (two-sided p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression, or the estimated coefficient is positive.
